case_id: LLT Wake Simulation - Velocity Cut Plane

pi_items.source: Verified against export_result.sh lines 21, which searches for files matching pattern 'wake_cutplane.*' with extensions .vtu, .vtr, .vtk, .pvd. Also verified against verifier.py line 54 which looks for 'wake_cutplane' VTK file.

Verified against task description (task.json line 7) which specifies saving project as '~/Documents/projects/llt_simulation.wpa'. Also verified against setup_task.sh line 13 and export_result.sh line 35 which look for this exact filename.

Appears in verifier.py line 65 as a comparison threshold. Standard VTK format (XML-based .vtu files) for structured velocity data: typical header ~1-2 KB, plus structured grid coordinates and vector data. A 10x10x10 grid with 3-component velocity vectors would be approximately 5-8 KB. However, no authoritative source found specifying exact minimum size for wake cut plane data. The value appears plausible but unverifiable from external sources.

Downloaded and extracted QBlade_linux_v0.96.2_64bit.zip from SourceForge (https://sourceforge.net/projects/qblade/files/QBlade_linux_v0.96.2_64bit.zip/download). Actual sample projects directory contains: NREL_5MW.wpa (729 KB), NREL_PhaseVI.wpa (591 KB), SANDIA_SERI-8.wpa (390 KB), TUB_BeRTwpa.wpa (1.3 MB). The file is named 'NREL_5MW.wpa', not 'NREL_5MW_Reference.wpa'.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses real wind turbine simulation software (QBlade v0.96.2) and real turbine specifications (NREL 5MW reference turbine). The NREL 5MW is a well-documented 5 MW offshore wind turbine baseline developed by NREL with rated wind speed 11.4 m/s and rotor speed 12.1 rpm. QBlade implements Lifting Line Theory (LLT) with explicit wake modeling using vortex rings. The VTK export is a real scientific data format. However, the metadata claim about the sample project filename is incorrect: the actual file is NREL_5MW.wpa (without 'Reference' in the name), which is included with QBlade v0.96.2 at 729 KB.
